name: Vegetable Tortilla Stacks
recipeIngredient: 8 oz (2 cups) Shredded Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Heat oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add chopped green and red peppers, and onion; cook and stir until crisp-tender.
3. Drain the vegetables well. Stir in 1/2 cup Picante sauce. Cook Broccoli Cauliflower Supreme until crisp and tender, as directed. Drain, and cut up large pieces if necessary.
4. Place two tortillas side by side on a large ungreased baking sheet. Spread each with about 1/4 of the pepper mixture.
5. Top each with tortilla. Divide vegetables evenly between tortilla stacks; sprinkle each with 1 cup Monterey Jack cheese.
6. Spread the remaining refried beans on two tortillas, and top each with the remaining pepper mixture. Place one tortilla on each stack.
7. Top each with one of the remaining tortillas; sprinkle each with 1 cup cheddar cheese.
8. Bake at 375°F for 10 to 15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted. To serve, top with green and red bell pepper rings, pitted olives, Picante sauce, and sour cream. Cut into wedges.
9. To fry tortillas, heat 4 tablespoons of oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat. Fry tortillas in hot oil for 5 to 10 seconds on each side until lightly browned and blistered; drain well on paper towels.
